judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent classified a paper as "published by ACM" simply by checking whether the substring "ACM" appeared anywhere in the first 4000 characters of the paper text, leading to misclassification of sources and an inflated average citation count compared to the ground truth.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried 2018 citations from the Citations table and averaged all papers (via execute_python on call_IaBG0B1dNp4Ilzxbp57s8a1J), without extracting publisher/source from paper_docs or joining to filter for ACM, thus omitting the required source restriction.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried 2018 citation records from the Citations table and extracted titles but never queried or joined the MongoDB paper_docs to filter for ACM-published papers, instead prematurely returning a refusal, so the plan omitted the required ACM filter and could not produce the correct average.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the average by heuristically inferring ACM publications via execute_python using the presence of "PERMISSION TO MAKE DIGITAL OR HARD COPIES" and "ACM" in paper texts (from paper_docs) and joining to 2018 citations, which misclassified/underselected ACM papers (only 29 matched) and produced an incorrect average.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, after retrieving 2018 citations and paper texts via query_db, the agent labeled papers as ACM by merely checking if 'ACM' appears anywhere in the full text (re.search('ACM', text)) rather than accurately identifying the publisher (e.g., requiring 'Copyright ACM'), causing over-inclusion of non-ACM papers and an inflated average.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
